heading: Control of transportation systems in time of war
label: 2644 Control of transportation systems in time of war
text: In time of war, the President, through the Secretary of Defense, may take possession and assume control of all or part of any system of transportation to transport troops, war material, and equipment, or for other purposes related to the emergency. So far as necessary, he may use the system to the exclusion of other traffic.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)474210:1361.(last par. under “Ordnance Department”), .Aug. 29, 1916, ch. 41839 Stat. 645The words “as may be needful or desirable” are omitted as surplusage.
Amendments1996— renumbered as this section and substituted “Secretary of Defense” for “Secretary of the Army”.Pub. L. 104–201section 4742 of this title
